libraryDescription:
The VCSSL Graphics2D library is one of the standard libraries for handling graphics in VCSSL,and provides animation-capable 2D computer graphics (2DCG) rendering functions.

Because the Graphics2D library is relatively large, it is difficult to use it effectively by referring only to the function specifications described here.For specific usage examples and guidance, refer to the "Official VCSSL 2DCG Development Guide."

function0.name: newGraphics2DRenderer
function0.declaration: int newGraphics2DRenderer( int width, int height, int graphicsID )
function0.description: Creates a 2DCG renderer and returns its unique identifier, called the renderer ID.
function0.parameters:
(int type) width : The width of the drawing area. 
(int type) height : The height of the drawing area. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource to use.

function1.name: deleteGraphics2DRenderer
function1.parameters: (int type) rendererID : The ID of the renderer to dispose.

function2.name: paintGraphics2D
function2.description: Composites sprites and renders the 2DCG scene.

function3.name: clearGraphics2D
function3.description: Clears the drawing contents with the background color.

function4.name: clearGraphics2D
function4.declaration: macro clearGraphics2D( int rendererID, bool removeAll )
function4.description: Clears the drawing contents with the background color and, if necessary, removes all mounted sprites.
function4.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(bool type) removeAll : Whether to remove all mounted sprites.

function5.name: setGraphics2DColor
function5.declaration: macro setGraphics2DColor( int rendererID, int red, int green, int blue, int alpha )
function5.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) red : The red component (0 to 255). 
(int type) green : The green component (0 to 255). 
(int type) blue : The blue component (0 to 255). 
(int type) alpha : The alpha component (0 to 255).

function6.name: setGraphics2DColor
function6.declaration: void setGraphics2DColor( int rendererID, int rgba[ ] )
function6.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[] type) rgba : The array storing the color components ([0]=red, [1]=green, [2]=blue, [3]=alpha, each ranging from 0 to 255).

function7.name: setGraphics2DSize
function7.declaration: macro setGraphics2DSize( int rendererID, int width, int height )
function7.description: Sets the size of the drawing area.
function7.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height.

function8.name: setDrawColor
function8.declaration: macro setDrawColor( int rendererID, int red, int green, int blue, int alpha )
function8.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) red : The red component (0 to 255). 
(int type) green : The green component (0 to 255). 
(int type) blue : The blue component (0 to 255). 
(int type) alpha : The alpha component (0 to 255).

function9.name: setDrawColor
function9.declaration: void setDrawColor( int rendererID, int rgba[ ] )
function9.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[] type) rgba : The array storing the color components ([0]=red, [1]=green, [2]=blue, [3]=alpha, each ranging from 0 to 255).

function10.name: setDrawFont
function10.declaration: void setDrawFont( int rendererID, string name )
function10.description: Sets the font used for text drawing.
function10.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(string type) name : The font name. This depends on the implementation and environment.

function11.name: setDrawFontSize
function11.declaration: void setDrawFontSize( int rendererID, int size )
function11.description: Sets the size of the font used for text drawing.
function11.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) size : The font size.

function12.name: setDrawFontBold
function12.declaration: void setDrawFontBold( int rendererID, bool isBold )
function12.description: Enables or disables bold style for the font used in text drawing. It cannot be used together with italic style.
function12.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(bool type) isBold : Whether bold style is enabled.

function13.name: setDrawFontItalic
function13.declaration: void setDrawFontItalic( int rendererID, bool isItalic )
function13.description: Enables or disables italic style for the font used in text drawing. It cannot be used together with bold style.
function13.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(bool type) isItalic : Whether italic style is enabled.

function14.name: drawPoint
function14.declaration: void drawPoint( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int radius, bool fill )
function14.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the center. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the center. 
(int type) radius : The radius. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the point.

function15.name: drawLine
function15.declaration: macro drawLine( int rendererID, int vertex_x1, int vertex_y1, int vertex_x2, int vertex_y2 )
function15.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x1 : The X coordinate of the first endpoint. 
(int type) vertex_y1 : The Y coordinate of the first endpoint. 
(int type) vertex_x2 : The X coordinate of the second endpoint. 
(int type) vertex_y2 : The Y coordinate of the second endpoint.

function16.name: drawLine
function16.declaration: macro drawLine( int rendererID, int vertex_x1, int vertex_y1, int vertex_x2, int vertex_y2, int lineWidth )
function16.description: Draws a straight line segment with an arbitrary thickness.
function16.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x1 : The X coordinate of the first endpoint. 
(int type) vertex_y1 : The Y coordinate of the first endpoint. 
(int type) vertex_x2 : The X coordinate of the second endpoint. 
(int type) vertex_y2 : The Y coordinate of the second endpoint. 
(int type) lineWidth : The line thickness.

function17.name: drawEllipse
function17.declaration: macro drawEllipse( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function17.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the ellipse.

function18.name: drawRectangle
function18.declaration: macro drawRectangle( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function18.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the rectangle.

function19.name: drawPolyline
function19.declaration: macro drawPolyline( int rendererID, int vertex_x[ ], int vertex_y[ ] )
function19.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[] type) vertex_x : The array storing the X coordinates of vertices. 
(int[] type) vertex_y : The array storing the Y coordinates of vertices.

function20.name: drawPolyline
function20.declaration: macro drawPolyline( int rendererID, int vertex_x[ ], int vertex_y[ ], int lineWidth )
function20.description: Draws a polyline with an arbitrary thickness.
function20.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[] type) vertex_x : The array storing the X coordinates of vertices. 
(int[] type) vertex_y : The array storing the Y coordinates of vertices. 
(int type) lineWidth : The line thickness.

function21.name: drawPolygon
function21.declaration: macro drawPolygon( int rendererID, int vertex_x[ ], int vertex_y[ ], bool fill )
function21.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[] type) vertex_x : The array storing the X coordinates of vertices. 
(int[] type) vertex_y : The array storing the Y coordinates of vertices. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the polygon.

function22.name: drawPolygon
function22.declaration: macro drawPolygon( int rendererID, int vertex_x[ ], int vertex_y[ ], int lineWidth )
function22.description: Draws a polygon with an arbitrary thickness.
function22.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[] type) vertex_x : The array storing the X coordinates of vertices. 
(int[] type) vertex_y : The array storing the Y coordinates of vertices. 
(int type) lineWidth : The line thickness.

function23.name: drawText
function23.declaration: macro drawText( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, string textString )
function23.description: Draws a single line of text.
function23.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the text starting position. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the text starting position. 
(string type) textString : The text content.

function24.name: drawText
function24.declaration: macro drawText( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int width, int height, string textString )
function24.description: Draws text over multiple lines with wrapping.
function24.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the text starting position. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the text starting position. 
(int type) width : The line width. 
(int type) height : The line height. 
(string type) textString : The text content.

function25.name: drawImage
function25.declaration: macro drawImage( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int graphicsID )
function25.description: Draws an image. The drawing result of another 2D or 3D renderer can also be composited and drawn as an image.
function25.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function26.name: drawImage
function26.declaration: macro drawImage( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int width, int height, int graphicsID )
function26.description: Draws an image after resizing it. The drawing result of another 2D or 3D renderer can also be composited and drawn as an image.
function26.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function27.name: drawImage
function27.declaration: void drawImage( int rendererID, int x, int y, int width, int height, int cropX, int cropY, int cropWidth, int cropHeight, int graphicsID )
function27.description: Draws a cropped portion of an image. The drawing result of another 2D or 3D renderer can also be composited and drawn as an image.In VCSSL 3.4.8 and earlier, the behavior of this function differed from the argument descriptions in the documentation.In VCSSL 3.4.10, the behavior was corrected to match the documentation.If you need the behavior of VCSSL 3.4.8 or earlier, replace calls to this function with calls to drawGraphics instead.
function27.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the drawing position. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the drawing position. 
(int type) width : The drawing width. 
(int type) height : The drawing height. 
(int type) cropX : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the source region. 
(int type) cropY : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the source region. 
(int type) cropWidth : The width of the source region. 
(int type) cropHeight : The height of the source region. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function28.name: setPixel
function28.declaration: macro setPixel( int rendererID, int red[ ][ ], int green[ ][ ], int blue[ ][ ], int alpha[ ][ ] )
function28.description: Directly sets the drawing contents from pixel color-component arrays.
function28.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[][] type) red : The array storing the red components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) green : The array storing the green components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) blue : The array storing the blue components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) alpha : The array storing the alpha components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function29.name: setPixel
function29.declaration: macro setPixel( int rendererID, int rgba[ ][ ][ ] )
function29.description: Directly sets the drawing contents from a pixel color-component array.
function29.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int[][][] type) rgba : The array storing the color components ([Y][X][0:red, 1:green, 2:blue, 3:alpha], values from 0 to 255).

function30.name: drawPixel
function30.declaration: void drawPixel( int rendererID, int x, int y, int red[ ][ ], int green[ ][ ], int blue[ ][ ], int alpha[ ][ ] )
function30.description: Draws the contents of pixel color-component arrays on top of the current drawing contents.
function30.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int[][] type) red : The array storing the red components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) green : The array storing the green components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) blue : The array storing the blue components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) alpha : The array storing the alpha components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function31.name: drawPixel
function31.declaration: void drawPixel( int rendererID, int x, int y, int rgba[ ][ ][ ] )
function31.description: Draws the contents of a pixel color-component array on top of the current drawing contents.
function31.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int[][][] type) rgba : The array storing the color components ([Y][X][0:red, 1:green, 2:blue, 3:alpha], values from 0 to 255).

function32.name: drawPixel
function32.declaration: void drawPixel( int rendererID, int x, int y, int width, int height, int red[ ][ ], int green[ ][ ], int blue[ ][ ], int alpha[ ][ ] )
function32.description: Resizes the contents of pixel color-component arrays and draws them on top of the current drawing contents.
function32.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height. 
(int[][] type) red : The array storing the red components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) green : The array storing the green components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) blue : The array storing the blue components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) alpha : The array storing the alpha components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function33.name: drawPixel
function33.declaration: void drawPixel( int rendererID, int x, int y, int width, int height, int rgba[ ][ ][ ] )
function33.description: Resizes the contents of a pixel color-component array and draws them on top of the current drawing contents.
function33.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height. 
(int[][][] type) rgba : The array storing the color components ([Y][X][0:red, 1:green, 2:blue, 3:alpha], values from 0 to 255).

function34.name: drawPixel
function34.declaration: void drawPixel( int rendererID, int x, int y, int width, int height, int baseX, int baseY, int baseWidth, int baseHeight, int red[ ][ ], int green[ ][ ], int blue[ ][ ], int alpha[ ][ ] )
function34.description: Extracts a specified region from pixel color-component arrays and draws it on top of the current drawing contents.
function34.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height. 
(int type) baseX : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the source region. 
(int type) baseY : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the source region. 
(int type) baseWidth : The width of the source region. 
(int type) baseHeight : The height of the source region. 
(int[][] type) red : The array storing the red components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) green : The array storing the green components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) blue : The array storing the blue components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255). 
(int[][] type) alpha : The array storing the alpha components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function35.name: drawPixel
function35.declaration: void drawPixel( int rendererID, int x, int y, int width, int height, int baseX, int baseY, int baseWidth, int baseHeight, int rgba[ ][ ][ ] )
function35.description: Extracts a specified region from a pixel color-component array and draws it on top of the current drawing contents.
function35.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height. 
(int type) baseX : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the source region. 
(int type) baseY : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the source region. 
(int type) baseWidth : The width of the source region. 
(int type) baseHeight : The height of the source region. 
(int[][][] type) rgba : The array storing the color components ([Y][X][0:red, 1:green, 2:blue, 3:alpha], values from 0 to 255).

function36.name: getPixel
function36.description: Converts the contents of the drawing area into a pixel color-component array and returns it.
function36.returnValue: (int[][][] type) The pixel color-component array ([Y][X][0:red, 1:green, 2:blue, 3:alpha], values from 0 to 255).

function37.name: getPixelRed
function37.description: Converts the contents of the drawing area into an array of red pixel components and returns it.
function37.returnValue: (int[][] type) The array of red pixel components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function38.name: getPixelGreen
function38.description: Converts the contents of the drawing area into an array of green pixel components and returns it.
function38.returnValue: (int[][] type) The array of green pixel components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function39.name: getPixelBlue
function39.description: Converts the contents of the drawing area into an array of blue pixel components and returns it.
function39.returnValue: (int[][] type) The array of blue pixel components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function40.name: getPixelAlpha
function40.description: Converts the contents of the drawing area into an array of alpha pixel components and returns it.
function40.returnValue: (int[][] type) The array of alpha pixel components ([Y][X], values from 0 to 255).

function41.name: newPointSprite
function41.declaration: int newPointSprite( int x, int y, int radius, bool fill )
function41.description: Creates a point sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function41.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) radius : The radius. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the sprite.

function42.name: newLineSprite
function42.declaration: int newLineSprite( int x1, int y1, int x2, int y2 )
function42.description: Creates a line-segment sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function42.parameters:
(int type) x1 : The X coordinate of the first endpoint. 
(int type) y1 : The Y coordinate of the first endpoint. 
(int type) x2 : The X coordinate of the second endpoint. 
(int type) y2 : The Y coordinate of the second endpoint.

function43.name: newEllipseSprite
function43.declaration: int newEllipseSprite( int x, int y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function43.description: Creates an ellipse sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function43.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the sprite.

function44.name: newRectangleSprite
function44.declaration: int newRectangleSprite( int x, int y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function44.description: Creates a rectangle sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function44.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the sprite.

function45.name: newPolylineSprite
function45.declaration: int newPolylineSprite( int x[ ], int y[ ] )
function45.description: Creates a polyline sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function45.parameters:
(int[] type) x : The array storing the X coordinates of vertices. 
(int[] type) y : The array storing the Y coordinates of vertices.

function46.name: newPolygonSprite
function46.declaration: int newPolygonSprite( int x[ ], int y[ ], bool fill )
function46.description: Creates a polygon sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function46.parameters:
(int[] type) x : The array storing the X coordinates of vertices. 
(int[] type) y : The array storing the Y coordinates of vertices. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the sprite.

function47.name: newImageSprite
function47.declaration: int newImageSprite( int x, int y, int width, int height, int graphicsID )
function47.description: Creates an image sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function47.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function48.name: newTextSprite
function48.declaration: int newTextSprite( int x, int y, int width, int height, string text )
function48.description: Creates a text sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.
function48.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the text starting position. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the text starting position. 
(int type) width : The line width. 
(int type) height : The line height. 
(string type) text : The text content.

function49.name: deleteSprite
function49.parameters: (int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite.

function50.name: mountSprite
function50.declaration: void mountSprite( int spriteID, int rendererID )
function50.description: Mounts a sprite onto a renderer.
function50.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID.

function51.name: demountSprite
function51.declaration: void demountSprite( int spriteID, int rendererID )
function51.description: Demounts a sprite from a renderer.
function51.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID.

function52.name: setSpriteDepth
function52.declaration: macro setSpriteDepth( int spriteID, double vertex_z )
function52.description: Sets the depth of a sprite. A larger depth value places the sprite farther back, and a smaller value places it closer to the front.
function52.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(double type) vertex_z : The depth.

function53.name: setSpriteLocation
function53.declaration: macro setSpriteLocation( int spriteID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y )
function53.description: Sets the position of a sprite.
function53.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner.

function54.name: setSpriteSize
function54.declaration: void setSpriteSize( int spriteID, int size )
function54.description: Sets the size of a sprite. This function is used for sprites whose size can be specified by a single parameter, such as point sprites.
function54.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) size : The radius.

function55.name: setSpriteSize
function55.declaration: macro setSpriteSize( int spriteID, int width, int height )
function55.description: Sets the size of a sprite.
function55.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height.

function56.name: setSpriteClip
function56.declaration: macro setSpriteClip( int spriteID, int clip_x, int clip_y, int clip_width, int clip_height )
function56.description: Sets the clipping information of a sprite.
function56.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) clip_x : The X coordinate of the clipping area. 
(int type) clip_y : The Y coordinate of the clipping area. 
(int type) clip_width : The width of the clipping area. 
(int type) clip_height : The height of the clipping area.

function57.name: setSpriteColor
function57.declaration: macro setSpriteColor( int spriteID, int red, int green, int blue, int alpha )
function57.description: Sets the color of a sprite.
function57.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) red : The red component (0 to 255). 
(int type) green : The green component (0 to 255). 
(int type) blue : The blue component (0 to 255). 
(int type) alpha : The alpha component (0 to 255).

function58.name: setSpriteColor
function58.declaration: void setSpriteColor( int spriteID, int rgba[ ] )
function58.description: Sets the color of a sprite.
function58.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int[] type) rgba : The array storing the color components ([0]=red, [1]=green, [2]=blue, [3]=alpha, each ranging from 0 to 255).

function59.name: setSpriteFill
function59.declaration: macro setSpriteFill( int spriteID, bool fillState )
function59.description: Sets whether the sprite is filled.
function59.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(bool type) fillState : Whether the sprite is filled.

function60.name: setSpriteText
function60.declaration: macro setSpriteText( int spriteID, string textString )
function60.description: Sets the text of a sprite.
function60.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(string type) textString : The text content.

function61.name: setSpriteImage
function61.declaration: macro setSpriteImage( int spriteID, int graphicsID )
function61.description: Sets the image of a sprite.
function61.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) graphicsID : The ID of the graphics resource storing the image.

function62.name: setSpriteFont
function62.declaration: void setSpriteFont( int spriteID, string name )
function62.description: Sets the font used for sprite text drawing.
function62.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(string type) name : The font name. This depends on the implementation and environment.

function63.name: setSpriteFontSize
function63.declaration: void setSpriteFontSize( int spriteID, int size )
function63.description: Sets the size of the font used for sprite text drawing.
function63.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) size : The font size.

function64.name: setSpriteFontBold
function64.declaration: void setSpriteFontBold( int spriteID, bool isBold )
function64.description: Enables or disables bold style for the font used in sprite text drawing. It cannot be used together with italic style.
function64.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(bool type) isBold : Whether bold style is enabled.

function65.name: setSpriteFontItalic
function65.declaration: void setSpriteFontItalic( int spriteID, bool isItalic )
function65.description: Enables or disables italic style for the font used in sprite text drawing. It cannot be used together with bold style.
function65.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(bool type) isItalic : Whether italic style is enabled.

function66.name: drawOval
function66.declaration: macro drawOval( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function66.description: Draws an ellipse.The newer drawEllipse function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function66.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the ellipse.

function67.name: drawRect
function67.declaration: macro drawRect( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function67.description: Draws a rectangle.The newer drawRectangle function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function67.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the rectangle.

function68.name: drawGraphics
function68.declaration: macro drawGraphics( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int graphicsID )
function68.description: Draws the contents of graphics data generated from the drawing result of another renderer or from an image file.The newer drawImage function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function68.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID.

function69.name: drawGraphics
function69.declaration: macro drawGraphics( int rendererID, int vertex_x, int vertex_y, int width, int height, int graphicsID )
function69.description: Draws resized contents of graphics data generated from the drawing result of another renderer or from an image file.The newer drawImage function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function69.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) vertex_x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) vertex_y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID.

function70.name: drawGraphics
function70.declaration: void drawGraphics( int rendererID, int fromLeftTopX, int fromLeftTopY, int fromRightBottomX, int fromRightBottmY, int toLeftTopX, int toLeftTopY, int toRightBottmX, int toRightBottomY, int graphicsID )
function70.description: Draws a specified region extracted from graphics data generated from the drawing result of another renderer or from an image file.The newer drawImage function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.In VCSSL 3.4.8 and earlier, the documented explanation of the arguments for this function was incorrect, so the documentation was corrected in VCSSL 3.4.10. Therefore, the behavior of this function itself has not changed.On the other hand, the newer drawImage function was changed so that its behavior matches the original documentation.Therefore, use this function when compatibility with older programs is important, and use drawImage in new programs.
function70.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) fromLeftTopX : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the cropped area in the source image. 
(int type) fromLeftTopY : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the cropped area in the source image. 
(int type) fromRightBottomX : The X coordinate of the lower-right corner of the cropped area in the source image. 
fromRightBottomY : The Y coordinate of the lower-right corner of the cropped area in the source image. 
(int type) toLeftTopX : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the drawing position. 
(int type) toLeftTopY : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the drawing position. 
toRightBottomX : The X coordinate of the lower-right corner of the drawing position. 
(int type) toRightBottomY : The Y coordinate of the lower-right corner of the drawing position. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID storing the source image.

function71.name: newOvalSprite
function71.declaration: int newOvalSprite( int x, int y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function71.description: Creates an ellipse sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.The newer newEllipseSprite function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function71.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the sprite.

function72.name: newRectSprite
function72.declaration: int newRectSprite( int x, int y, int width, int height, bool fill )
function72.description: Creates a rectangle sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.The newer newRectangleSprite function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function72.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(bool type) fill : Whether to fill the sprite.

function73.name: newGraphicsSprite
function73.declaration: int newGraphicsSprite( int x, int y, int width, int height, int graphicsID )
function73.description: Creates an image sprite and returns its unique identifier, called the sprite ID.The newer newImageSprite function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function73.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner. 
(int type) width : The width of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) height : The height of the bounding rectangle. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID.

function74.name: addSprite
function74.declaration: void addSprite( int spriteID, int rendererID )
function74.description: Mounts a sprite. The newer mountSprite function has been supported since VCSSL 3. This function is supported for compatibility.
function74.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID.

function75.name: removeSprite
function75.declaration: void removeSprite( int spriteID, int rendererID )
function75.description: Demounts a sprite. The newer demountSprite function has been supported since VCSSL 3. This function is supported for compatibility.
function75.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID.

function76.name: setSpriteGraphics
function76.declaration: macro setSpriteGraphics( int spriteID, int graphicsID )
function76.description: Sets the graphics data of a sprite.The newer setSpriteImage function has been supported since VCSSL 3.3.24.This function is supported for compatibility.
function76.parameters:
(int type) spriteID : The ID of the target sprite. 
(int type) graphicsID : The graphics resource ID.
